text:
There once was a young hare who dreamt of becoming a great singer. Every night, before drifting off to sleep, the hare would hum a song about flowers blooming and sunbeam showers. Years passed, and this little hare didn't grow any bigger. One day, it found an old flute tucked away in a forgotten chest.

Taking it home, the hare practiced. Each morning, it would awaken the sun with a sweet melody. Word spread; birds began to sing earlier, flowers blossomed more vibrantly. Even the stones chimed a little tune. It became known as the Spring Songster.

The rabbit became famous, and townspeople traveled from far and wide to hear his song. There were parties just to listen. One evening, after performing in an enchanted forest filled with glowing mushrooms, a noble duck invited him to a royal banquet. There, amidst glittering jewels and fine food, the hare sang his greatest ballad—a narrative about love, loss, and perseverance that lulled
